title: buildkit: add support for persistent caching across builds with `--mount=type=cache`
body:
Following PR introduces a new mount `type=cache` in parity to buildkit
which allows users to share persistent cache between different builds.

Allowing users to cache content generated by business logic or enhance
build performance by caching components across various builds.

Closes: https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues/3452

comments.body:
https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/docs/Containerfile.5.md needs update.

The current text there “Current supported mount TYPES are bind, and tmpfs.” has a comma too much.

Moreover later one more TYPE is introduced: secret.  Now one can say tmpfs and bind are real mounts, while --mount=type=secret is a fake mount, but I thinks it better if either all mounts are listed one place, or no place lists all mount types.
I agree humans don't care about the difference. they should all be listed together.

> https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/main/docs/Containerfile.5.md needs update.
> 
> The current text there “Current supported mount TYPES are bind, and tmpfs.” has a comma too much.
> 
> Moreover later one more TYPE is introduced: secret. Now one can say tmpfs and bind are real mounts, while --mount=type=secret is a fake mount, but I thinks it better if either all mounts are listed one place, or no place lists all mount types.

@dilyanpalauzov Thanks added `secrets` in `--mount` as well but i think there is separate section for secrets  cause it is always used in combination to `buildah build --secret` hence a seperate section explains the usage instructions however work like pretty much regular filesystem mounts.
